quoteFirst: Jumping out of the train, I saw a notice-board bearing the words, "Belfort direction,
quoteLast: Platform No. 3." I proceeded to the _trottoir_ mentioned, and waited for the hour of the train's departure. At half-past eleven the _employés_ got together a long string of cattle trucks. I was at a loss what to do, whether to get into one of them or ask the men if that was really the train for Belfort. In the midst of my speculation, and as though they had understood my dilemma, the railway officials put up five or six notices similar to the one I had seen on arriving.
title:
True Stories of the Great War, Volume 4 (of 6)
